summary: Joel briefly recommends SpamBayes as an effective spam filtering solution, noting it catches over 200 spam emails per day with virtually no false positives. He credits Paul Graham with inventing Bayesian filtering and points readers to open source implementations.
key_points: SpamBayes effectively filters 200+ spam emails per day with near-zero false positives
Bayesian filtering, credited to Paul Graham, is positioned as the best spam solution available at the time
Open source implementations make Bayesian filtering widely accessible
money_quotes: My incoming spam is running at over 200 junk emails a day, but SpamBayes is catching them all, with virtually no false positives.
Bayesian filtering, invented by Paul Graham and available in many open source implementations, is the best answer yet.
key_insight: Bayesian spam filtering, as implemented by SpamBayes, was remarkably effective at handling high volumes of spam with virtually no false positives.
